annotations.label: Rainfall data example

anchor.snippet: Suppose we have daily rainfall data

annotations.note: Illustrative example; not present as a formalized instance in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conditional expectation given an event

anchor.snippet: the conditional expectation of X given A is

annotations.note: E[X|A] itself is not a named Mathlib definition; it is expressible as an integral against the conditioned measure `ProbabilityTheory.cond`.

annotations.label: Conditional expectation for discrete random variables

anchor.snippet: the conditional expectation of X given Y is

annotations.note: Mathlib has no per-value E[X|Y=y] discrete formula; it defines the σ-algebra-valued `condExp` from which the discrete formula is a special case.

annotations.label: Conditional expectation for continuous random variables
annotations.note: The density-based E[X|Y=y] formula is not defined directly; Mathlib provides the regular conditional distribution `condDistrib` from which it derives.

annotations.label: Conditional expectation of L^2 random variables

anchor.snippet: The conditional expectation of X is defined analogously

annotations.note: `condExpL2` is the orthogonal-projection L² conditional expectation.

annotations.label: Non-uniqueness with constant Y

anchor.snippet: Consider the case where Y is the constant random variable that is always 1

annotations.note: Motivating example for non-uniqueness; not a Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Non-uniqueness with 2-dimensional Y

anchor.snippet: Consider the case where Y is the 2-dimensional random vector

annotations.note: Motivating example only; no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of conditional expectation up to measure zero

anchor.snippet: Conditional expectation is unique up to a set of measure zero

annotations.note: Characterizes a.e. uniqueness of `condExp` from the defining set-integral property.

annotations.label: Existence via Hilbert projection
annotations.note: `condExpL2` is literally defined as `orthogonalProjectionOnto` the `lpMeas` subspace via the Hilbert projection theorem.

annotations.label: Conditional expectation equals linear regression for jointly normal variables

anchor.snippet: An important special case is when X and Y are jointly normally distributed

annotations.note: Mathlib has no multivariate Gaussian and hence no formalization of the jointly-normal linear-regression identity.

annotations.label: Conditional expectation given a sub-sigma-algebra

anchor.section: Conditional expectation with respect to a sub- σ -algebra
anchor.snippet: A conditional expectation of X given

annotations.note: `condExp m μ f` is exactly the conditional expectation of `f` w.r.t. the sub-σ-algebra `m`.

annotations.label: Existence via Radon–Nikodym
annotations.note: Identifies `condExp` a.e. with the Radon–Nikodym derivative of `μ.withDensity f` restricted to the sub-σ-algebra.

annotations.label: Conditional expectation given a random variable

anchor.section: Conditional expectation with respect to a random variable
anchor.snippet: The conditional expectation of X given Y is defined by applying the above construction

annotations.note: Mathlib expresses E[X|Y] via `condExp (MeasurableSpace.comap Y ‹_›) μ X`; there is no dedicated `condExp Y` abbreviation.

annotations.label: Doob–Dynkin lemma representation

anchor.section: Conditional expectation with respect to a random variable

annotations.note: Factorization of a σ(Y)-measurable function as a measurable function of Y — the Doob–Dynkin lemma.

annotations.label: Almost-sure uniqueness of conditional expectation

anchor.snippet: Uniqueness can be shown to be almost sure

annotations.note: Same a.e. uniqueness characterization from the set-integral condition.

annotations.label: Markov kernel property of conditional probability

anchor.snippet: It can be shown that they form a Markov kernel

annotations.note: `condExpKernel` is defined and carries an `IsMarkovKernel` instance.

annotations.label: Law of the unconscious statistician

anchor.snippet: The Law of the unconscious statistician is then

annotations.note: Expresses `μ[f | m]` a.e. as an integral against the conditional-expectation kernel.

annotations.label: General definition of conditional expectation in Banach space
annotations.note: `condExp` is defined for functions into any Banach space `E` (`NormedAddCommGroup E`, `NormedSpace ℝ E`).

annotations.label: Pulling out independent factors
annotations.note: For `m₁`-measurable `f` with `m₁, m₂` independent, `μ[f | m₂] = μ[f]` a.e.

annotations.label: Stability under measurability
annotations.note: If `f` is already `m`-strongly measurable and integrable, `μ[f | m] = f` a.e.

annotations.label: Pulling out known factors
annotations.note: `m`-measurable factor `f` can be pulled out: `μ[f*g | m] = f * μ[g | m]` a.e.; there is also a bilinear form.

annotations.label: Law of total expectation
annotations.note: ∫ μ[f|m] dμ = ∫ f dμ.

annotations.label: Tower property
annotations.note: For `m₁ ≤ m₂`, `μ[μ[f|m₂]|m₁] = μ[f|m₁]` a.e.

annotations.label: Doob martingale property
annotations.note: `fun i => μ[f | ℱ i]` is a martingale — the Doob (Lévy) martingale.

annotations.label: Linearity of conditional expectation
annotations.note: `condExp_add` plus `condExp_smul`/`condExp_sub` give linearity.

annotations.label: Monotonicity
annotations.note: `f ≤ᵐ g → μ[f|m] ≤ᵐ μ[g|m]` for integrable `f, g`.

annotations.label: Conditional monotone convergence
annotations.note: No conditional monotone convergence theorem for `condExp` was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conditional dominated convergence
annotations.note: Mathlib has an L¹-convergence version of dominated convergence for `condExpL1`, but not the pointwise-a.e. conditional DCT statement of the article.

annotations.label: Conditional Fatou's lemma
annotations.note: No conditional Fatou lemma was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conditional Jensen's inequality
annotations.note: `φ(μ[f|m]) ≤ᵐ μ[φ ∘ f | m]` for convex `φ`.

annotations.label: Conditional variance
annotations.note: `condVar` is the conditional variance `Var[X; μ | m]`.

annotations.label: Algebraic formula for the variance
annotations.note: `Var[X|m] =ᵐ μ[X²|m] − (μ[X|m])²`.

annotations.label: Law of total variance
annotations.note: `E[Var[X|m]] + Var[E[X|m]] = Var[X]`.

annotations.label: Martingale convergence
annotations.note: L¹ (Lévy-type) martingale convergence: `μ[g | ℱ n]` converges a.e. to `μ[g | ⨆ n, ℱ n]`; `Submartingale.ae_tendsto_limitProcess` is the underlying submartingale form.

annotations.label: Conditional expectation as L^2-projection
annotations.note: `condExpL2` is defined as the orthogonal projection onto `lpMeas E 𝕜 m 2 μ`.

annotations.label: Self-adjointness of conditional expectation
annotations.note: ⟨E[f|m], g⟩ = ⟨f, E[g|m]⟩ in L², the self-adjointness identity.

annotations.label: Contractive projection on L^p
annotations.note: Mathlib has contractivity for p=1 (`eLpNorm_one_condExp_le_eLpNorm`) and p=2 (`norm_condExpL2_le_one`), but not a uniform L^p (1 ≤ p ≤ ∞) statement.

annotations.label: Doob's conditional independence property
annotations.note: Mathlib defines `CondIndep`/`iCondIndep` but no theorem stating Doob's conditional-independence characterization of the conditional expectation was found.
